Question: A boy jumps on a merry-go-round with a radius of 5 m that is at rest. It starts accelerating at a constant rate up to an angular velocity of 5 rad/s in 20 seconds. What is the distance travelled by the boy?
Answer: The angular acceleration is $$\alpha = \frac{( 5.0 - 0 ) \text{rad} \text{/} \text{s}}{20.0 \text{s}} = 0.25 \text{rad} \text{/} \text{s}^{2}$$ . Therefore, the total angle that the boy passes through is $$\text{Delta} \theta = \frac{\upomega^{2} - \upomega_{0}^{2}}{2 \alpha} = \frac{( 5.0 )^{2} - 0}{2 ( 0.25 )} = 50 \text{rad}$$ . Thus, we calculate $$s = r \theta = 5.0 \text{m} ( 50.0 \text{rad} ) = 250.0 \text{m}$$ .
